title: Doc-Init Rec#4 — E2E tests on werewolf-minigame-stub fixture
description: Add 3 end-to-end test files covering the complete fab init extension from TASK-002/003/004. Tests run against examples/werewolf-minigame-stub as the target fixture. Tests cover: (1) forensic.json production with correct framework fields, (2) .claude/ infrastructure installation (SKILL.md + hook + settings.json merge), (3) non-destructive idempotency (second fab init run ABORTs/skips correctly).

files.change: Test using examples/werewolf-minigame-stub as target directory; calls initFabric(targetDir) or equivalent; asserts .fabric/forensic.json exists; asserts forensic.json.framework.kind === 'cocos-creator'; asserts forensic.json.framework.version === '3.8.0'; asserts forensic.json.entry_points.length >= 2 including 'assets/scripts/Game.ts'; asserts forensic.json passes ForensicReport zod schema validation

Test using fresh temp copy of werewolf-minigame-stub; calls initFabric(targetDir); asserts .claude/skills/agents-md-init/SKILL.md exists and is non-empty; asserts .claude/hooks/agents-md-init-reminder.cjs exists; reads .claude/settings.json and asserts Stop hook entry for agents-md-init-reminder is present; validates settings.json is valid JSON

files.target: E2E test: idempotency and non-destructive behavior
files.change: Test 1: call initFabric twice on same target — verify second call ABORTs with message referencing forensic.json already existing; verify .fabric/ files unchanged (no duplicate writes). Test 2: pre-create .claude/skills/agents-md-init/SKILL.md with custom content, run initFabric — verify SKILL.md content unchanged (skip, not overwrite). Test 3: pre-create .claude/settings.json with a different Stop hook, run initFabric — verify both hooks present in output settings.json

implementation: Set up test helpers: copyFixture(src, dest) to clone werewolf-minigame-stub to a temp dir (vitest temp or os.tmpdir); cleanupFixture(dir) for afterEach cleanup
Write init-forensic.test.ts: import ForensicReport from @fabric/shared; import initFabric from commands/init.ts; run against fresh temp fixture; parse forensic.json and use zod.safeParse to validate
Write init-claude-install.test.ts: assert file existence with fs.existsSync; parse settings.json and check hooks.Stop array for agents-md-init-reminder entry
Write init-nondestructive.test.ts: implement all 3 idempotency test cases; use jest/vitest mock or spy to capture ABORT messages if thrown as errors or logged

reference.pattern: E2E CLI test with fixture directory
reference.examples: Check existing test files in packages/cli/tests/ for test framework (vitest vs jest) and fixture patterns used

criteria: pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test — all 3 new test files pass
init-forensic.test.ts: forensic.json schema validation passes via ForensicReport.safeParse()
init-claude-install.test.ts: all 3 .claude/ artifacts present after initFabric
init-nondestructive.test.ts: second initFabric does not modify already-present files

rationale.chosen_approach: E2E tests against a real fixture (werewolf-minigame-stub) provide the highest confidence for init flow correctness; 3 separate test files match 3 distinct concern areas
alternatives_considered: Unit tests mocking fs operations (faster but misses integration of multiple modules)
Single test file (harder to navigate and maintain)
decision_factors: werewolf-minigame-stub is already in examples/ as a canonical Cocos 3.x test target
3 test files mirror the 3 Recs being tested
rationale.tradeoffs: E2E tests are slower than unit tests; run after unit tests in CI pipeline

manual_checks: pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test — verify all 3 test files show green
Review test output for asserting correct framework fields (cocos-creator 3.8.0)
success_metrics: 3 test files: init-forensic.test.ts, init-claude-install.test.ts, init-nondestructive.test.ts all pass
Zero flaky tests (temp dir cleanup works correctly)

risks.description: Temp directory cleanup races in parallel test runs
risks.mitigation: Use unique temp dir per test (randomUUID suffix); always cleanup in afterEach/finally block
risks.fallback: Disable parallel test execution for init tests with vitest --pool=forks if races occur
